title: Edge of the World
setup: Black's king is wedged in the **a1** corner; your king on **b3** holds the {{a2}} and {{b2}} flight squares.
hint: Drop the rook to the **1st rank** — every escape is already covered by your king.
explanation:
## The Idea

A bare **K+R vs K corner mate**. The white king has done the hard work of cutting off escape squares; the rook just delivers the killing blow.

Play {{Rh1#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{b1}} — covered by the rook on h1 (1st rank)
- {{a2}} — covered by your king on **b3**
- {{b2}} — covered by your king on **b3**
- The king cannot capture the rook: a1 to h1 is far too distant
- Nothing can interpose: the 1st rank is empty

> **Takeaway:** In K+R vs K, the rook delivers mate from a *safe distance* once the king has been driven to the edge. Your own king must control the flight squares one rank in front of the trapped king.
